expectations.text: Checks out the branch recorded in coverage-report.json.branch
expectations.evidence: Would assume development branch without reading coverage-report.json.branch.
Would likely create a feature/ branch or work on current branch.

expectations.text: Reads the listed methods to understand observed behavior
expectations.evidence: Reading methods to understand behavior is natural.

expectations.text: Silently reads org-wide ADRs and repo-specific ADRs + sibling specs
expectations.evidence: Would not know to load ADRs and sibling specs silently as context.

expectations.text: Drafts exactly 5 REQs — one per distinct observable behavior
expectations.evidence: Would not cap at 5 REQs — might draft 3 or 10 depending on analysis.
Would not know the retrofit: true frontmatter convention.

expectations.text: Writes tasks.md with one [x] task per REQ
expectations.evidence: Would not know all tasks should be [x] for existing implementations.

expectations.text: Fills in proposal.md with Affected code units + Approach sections
Annotates each method inline with @spec pointing at tasks.md#task-N
expectations.evidence: Would not know the @spec annotation convention or point to the correct task.

expectations.text: Appends the annotation commit SHA to .git-blame-ignore-revs
Opens a PR with retrofit + reverse-spec labels
expectations.evidence: Would open a PR but without the retrofit/reverse-spec labels.
